reasoning: Since June 2025, Trump has teased the announcement ('soon'), but failed to pull the trigger. By October 2025, his urgency decreased to a non-committal 'might be made by the end of the year.' While the 'shadow chair' idea provides incentive for an early announcement, historically Fed chairs are nominated 3-6 months before the transition. A May 2026 swap gives plenty of time through Q1 2026. Given the lack of a forcing mechanism before Dec 31 and Trump's track record of delaying personnel decisions, I estimate a 66% chance the announcement slips into 2026.
batch_queries: Will Trump refrain from announcing a next Fed Chair in 2025?
